document.title: Usability Engineering Report for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability Engineering Report A-UER-001 Rev.5 for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents usability engineering, application specification, hazards and hazardous situations related to usability, usability verification, usability validation, accompanying documents, and training for the X-ray detector.
keyPoints: The report identifies the document as File No A-UER-001, Rev. No 5, Rev. Date Dec 01, 2022.
The intended purpose states HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are for digital imaging in general radiographic systems for human anatomy and are not to be used for mammography.
Normative references listed are EN ISO 14971:2019 and EN 62366-1:2015.
The intended user is a medical professional with a professional license, familiar with the user manual, with Korean language understanding.
Frequently used functions include reading the user manual, connecting power, data, LAN, trigger, and communication cables.
Usability validation reports that all users succeeded within the acceptable range and were accepted as green zones.

evidence.claim: The document is File No A-UER-001, Rev. No 5, dated Dec 01, 2022.
evidence.evidenceText: File No A-UER-001 Rev. No 5 Rev. Date Dec 01, 2022

evidence.claim: The report is for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
evidence.evidenceText: For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC HAD1717MG

evidence.claim: The document overview describes the document as a usability engineering report for an X-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: This document is the Usability Engineering Report for X-ray detector.

evidence.claim: The report cites EN ISO 14971:2019 for risk management to medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2019, Medical devices – Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The report cites EN 62366-1:2015 for usability engineering to medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62366-1:2015 - Medical devices - Application of usability engineering to medical devices

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic digital imaging for human anatomy, replacing film or screen based radiographic systems, excluding mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG are indicated for digital imaging solution designed for general radiographic system for human anatomy. It is intended to replace film or screen based radiographic systems in all general purpose diagnostic procedures. Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The patient population is radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, chest, spinal, abdominal, hand, foot, and internal organs.
evidence.evidenceText: Radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, Chest, spinal, abdominal, particular, hand, foot and other internal organs.

evidence.claim: The intended user is a licensed medical professional familiar with the user manual and Korean language understanding.
evidence.evidenceText: Education: A medical professional with a professional license... familiar with user manual... Language Understanding: Korean

evidence.claim: Operating condition limits are ambient temperature +5 to 40℃ and relative humidity 30 to 75 RH % without condensation.
evidence.evidenceText: Operating condition Limit - Ambient temperature range:+5 ~ 40℃ - Relative humidity:30 ~ 75 RH % without condensation

evidence.claim: Storage condition limits are ambient temperature -10 to +50℃ and relative humidity 10 to 80 RH % without condensation.
evidence.evidenceText: Storage condition Limit - Ambient temperature range:-10 ~ + 50℃ - Relative humidity:10 ~ 80 RH % without condensation

evidence.claim: The report states the classification of applied parts is not applied part.
evidence.evidenceText: 6.1.5 Classification of APPLIED PARTS Not applied part

evidence.claim: Frequently used functions include reading the user manual and connecting power, data, LAN, trigger, and communication cables.
evidence.evidenceText: Frequently Used functions... Familiarize yourself with the user manual... Connecting the power cord... Connecting the Data Cable... Connecting the LAN cable... Trigger cable connection... Connecting the communication cable

evidence.claim: Known or foreseeable usability hazards include susceptibility to environmental influences, use with power supply, need for calibration, provision of safety information in the user manual, use in X-ray installed hospitals, accessories, radiation workers, and portability.
evidence.evidenceText: The equipment is susceptible to environmental influences... It is used with power supply... need calibration... User manual will be provided with safety information... environment where x-ray installed hospitals... power supply, connector etc... used by radiation workers... portable for various applications.

evidence.claim: Possible use errors include proceeding without knowledge of the manual, mounting power cord or cable with wet hands, and incorrect detector connection cable insertion.
evidence.evidenceText: The user performs the procedure without the user's knowledge of the manual... mounts the power cord with wet hands... mounts the cable with wet hands... Insert the power box to the detector connection cable incorrectly.

evidence.claim: Primary operating functions include patient preparation, power cord mounting, cable mounting, fitting detector in normal position, software run, X-ray survey, power off, power cord removal, quality guarantee, and maintenance.
evidence.evidenceText: characteristics related to SAFETY... patient preparation... Power cord mounting... Cable Code Mounting... Fit Detector in Normal Position... Run Software... X-ray Survey... Power Off with the Power button... Power cord removal... Quality guaranteed... Maintenance

evidence.claim: The usability validation plan used five typical intended users who can use radiation devices, such as doctors or radiologists.
evidence.evidenceText: Typical Intended User (Test Staff) : 5 persons... those who can use radiation devices (doctors or radiologists)

evidence.claim: The usability validation acceptance criterion identifies green zone as widely acceptable Level 1 and red zone as unacceptable Level 2 to 16.
evidence.evidenceText: Green Zone, Widely Acceptable Zone: [Level 1] Red zone, unacceptable zone: [Level 2~16]

evidence.claim: The report states tests were conducted according to the usability validation plan and all users were successful in the acceptable range.
evidence.evidenceText: Tests were conducted according to the test methods established in the usability validation plan, and all users were successful in the acceptable range of the test criteria. Therefore, all users were accepted as green zones.

evidence.claim: Accompanying documents are Usability Engineering Plan A-UEP-001, Risk management report A-RMR-0-01, and TCF TCF-001.
evidence.evidenceText: Accompanying document 1. Usability Engineering Plan (A-UEP-001) 2. Risk management report (A-RMR-0-01) 3. TCF (TCF-001)

evidence.claim: If training is required, training materials, access to training materials, or training should be provided.
evidence.evidenceText: If training is required... at least one of the following should be done: - Provide training materials - Ensuring access to training materials - Training is provided.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is not stated in the source text; authoritySignal says FDA, but FDA does not appear in the document text.
Source metadata productSignal 'HAD/A/F 1417-1717' does not match the product models explicitly stated in the report text, HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.

The source contains conflicting product/use evidence: section 6.11.1 states the device applies heat to the human body to relieve muscle pain, and section 6.11.2 mentions a medical warmer BPD-1700 G, while the report title and intended purpose identify an X-ray detector.
Product model naming is inconsistent in the validation plan text, including 'HAD1717MG (HAD17MC)' and 'HAD1717MG (HAD1717MC)'.

OCR or source quality appears imperfect, with terms such as 'IEC62336', 'primary motor function', 'data code', and 'information on stability' appearing inconsistent with surrounding usability engineering terminology.
